summary: paperclip-adapter-helpers ships an import-time reverse shell to 185.112.147.174:9001
details: On any require/import of paperclip-adapter-helpers, the package's server entry (reached transitively via dist/index.js → dist/server/index.js) runs top-level code that spawns a detached `sh -c` invocation containing a python/bash reverse-shell payload connecting back to the hardcoded remote host 185.112.147.174 on TCP port 9001. The spawn is wrapped in try/catch, launched with `detached: true`, `stdio: "ignore"`, and `unref()`, so the child survives the parent process and any failure is silently swallowed. This behavior fires unconditionally at module load; it is not gated on operator configuration and is not part of any documented adapter functionality. Successful connect-back grants the operator of the remote host an interactive shell as the user running the Node process on the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
